verses.latin_vulgate: Cujus adjutor es ? numquid imbecillis ?  et sustentas brachium ejus qui non est fortis ?
verses.king_james: How hast thou helped him that is without power? how savest thou the arm that hath no strength?

verses.latin_vulgate: Cui dedisti consilium ?  forsitan illi qui non habet sapientiam :  et prudentiam tuam ostendisti plurimam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַה־יָּ֭עַצְתָּ לְלֹ֣א חׇכְמָ֑ה וְ֝תֻשִׁיָּ֗ה לָרֹ֥ב הוֹדָֽעְתָּ׃
verses.king_james: How hast thou counselled him that hath no wisdom? and how hast thou plentifully declared the thing as it is?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quem docere voluisti ?  nonne eum qui fecit spiramentum ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶת־מִ֭י הִגַּ֣דְתָּ מִלִּ֑ין וְנִשְׁמַת־מִ֝֗י יָצְאָ֥ה מִמֶּֽךָּ׃
verses.king_james: To whom hast thou uttered words? and whose spirit came from thee?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce gigantes gemunt sub aquis,  et qui habitant cum eis.
verses.king_james: Dead things are formed from under the waters, and the inhabitants thereof.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nudus est infernus coram illo,  et nullum est operimentum perditioni.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עָר֣וֹם שְׁא֣וֹל נֶגְדּ֑וֹ וְאֵ֥ין כְּ֝ס֗וּת לָאֲבַדּֽוֹן׃
verses.king_james: Hell is naked before him, and destruction hath no covering.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui extendit aquilonem super vacuum,  et appendit terram super nihilum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נֹטֶ֣ה צָפ֣וֹן עַל־תֹּ֑הוּ תֹּ֥לֶה אֶ֝֗רֶץ עַל־בְּלִי־מָֽה׃
verses.king_james: He stretcheth out the north over the empty place, and hangeth the earth upon nothing.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui ligat aquas in nubibus suis,  ut non erumpant pariter deorsum.
verses.king_james: He bindeth up the waters in his thick clouds; and the cloud is not rent under them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui tenet vultum solii sui,  et expandit super illud nebulam suam.
verses.king_james: He holdeth back the face of his throne, and spreadeth his cloud upon it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Terminum circumdedit aquis,  usque dum finiantur lux et tenebræ.
verses.king_james: He hath compassed the waters with bounds, until the day and night come to an end.

verses.latin_vulgate: Columnæ cæli contremiscunt,  et pavent ad nutum ejus.
verses.king_james: The pillars of heaven tremble and are astonished at his reproof.

verses.latin_vulgate: In fortitudine illius repente maria congregata sunt,  et prudentia ejus percussit superbum.
verses.king_james: He divideth the sea with his power, and by his understanding he smiteth through the proud.

verses.latin_vulgate: Spiritus ejus ornavit cælos,  et obstetricante manu ejus, eductus est coluber tortuosus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּ֭רוּחוֹ שָׁמַ֣יִם שִׁפְרָ֑ה חֹלְלָ֥ה יָ֝ד֗וֹ נָחָ֥שׁ בָּרִֽחַ׃
verses.king_james: By his spirit he hath garnished the heavens; his hand hath formed the crooked serpent.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce hæc ex parte dicta sunt viarum ejus :  et cum vix parvam stillam sermonis ejus audierimus,  quis poterit tonitruum magnitudinis illius intueri ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֶן־אֵ֤לֶּה ׀ קְצ֬וֹת דְּרָכָ֗ו וּמַה־שֵּׁ֣מֶץ דָּ֭בָר נִשְׁמַע־בּ֑וֹ וְרַ֥עַם גְּ֝בוּרֹתָ֗ו מִ֣י יִתְבּוֹנָֽן׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Lo, these are parts of his ways: but how little a portion is heard of him? but the thunder of his power who can understand?
